Serving Communities

About Us

Mwinja Divine Help Foundation

A Refugee-Led Organization created in 2023 in Kampala, Uganda. It was founded in response to the increasing social, economic, and protection issues that refugees and host communities face. The organization works in areas where poverty, unemployment, juvenile delinquency, and inequality are prevalent, and vulnerable groups—particularly women and children—face increased risks of marginalization, violence, and financial difficulties.

is committed to improving people’s living conditions by promoting access to education, health, art, culture and local development.

is a non-profit organization dedicated to humanitarian causes and social development. It is characterized by charity, compassion and solidarity.
The organization does not limit itself to social work but also includes socio-cultural, economic and financial aspects, as well as development activities.

Our Vision

Socially and economically empowered communities

Our Mission

Sustainable livelihoods for all

Learn More About Us
What We Do

Our Programs

works to transform lives by promoting consling growth, empowering vulnerable individuals, and strengthening communities through inclusive development initiatives. Through community outreach, awareness campaigns, and collaborative partnerships, the foundation strives to cultivate harmony, peace, and lasting hope among disadvantaged populations across Uganda.

Education Programs

Empowering refugees through adult literacy, vocational training, and capacity building.

Learn More

Economic Empowerment

Building sustainable incomes and financial independence.

Learn More

Urban Refugee Livelihoods

Empowering refugees to build independent urban lives.

Learn More

Skills Development

Practical vocational training for self-reliance.

Learn More

Financial Inclusion

Savings, credit and financial literacy.

Learn More

Rights & Advocacy

Protecting human rights and dignity.

Learn More

Sporting and Artistic

Promoting sports, creativity, and youth talent development for community well-being.

Learn More

Mental Health and Disability Support

Providing psychosocial support and promoting inclusion for vulnerable groups.

Learn More

Culinary Art

Training in cooking skills, food safety, and culinary entrepreneurship.

Learn More

Agriculture Programs

Supporting sustainable farming, food security, and rural livelihoods.

Learn More

Fishing Program

Promoting sustainable fishing practices and aquaculture for food security and income generation.

Learn More

Volunteers

The Uganda Context

Uganda hosts approximately 1.96 million refugees and asylum seekers as of October 2025—the largest refugee population in Africa.

With 55% from South Sudan and 31% from the Democratic Republic of Congo, the country demonstrates remarkable hospitality through progressive refugee policies.

However, urban refugees in Kampala face overcrowding, limited livelihood opportunities, language barriers, and restricted healthcare access despite expectations for self-reliance.

Organization Snapshot

Founded: 2023

Registered: 2025 (NGO Bureau)

Type: Refugee-Led Organization

Location: Rubaga Division, Kampala

Target Population

70% Refugees30% Hosts

Focus Areas: Education, Health, Livelihoods, Advocacy and Capacity Building

Get Involved
“Mwinja Divine Help Foundation changed my life. Before, I had no hope. Now I have skills, I have work, and I can support my family. They gave me a second chance.”

Amina K.

Program Beneficiary

Kampala, Uganda

Share your skills and time to make a direct impact. From teaching to community outreach, your contribution matters.

Explore Partnerships

Donate

Your generous contributions enable us to continue our vital work and reach more families in need.

Make a Donation

Strategic Goal

To promote social and economic empowerment of refugees and low-income host communities through inclusive participation and sustainable livelihood initiatives.

Target Population

We serve both refugee and host communities using a 70/30 approach to ensure inclusion and sustainability.

Refugees 70%Hosts 30%
  • • Women & adolescent girls (economic empowerment)
  • • Out-of-school youth (skills & livelihoods)
  • • Adult learners (literacy, language, digital skills)
  • • Vulnerable households (food security & protection)

Refugee Demographics in Uganda

South Sudan55%
DR Congo31%
Somalia3%
Burundi3%
Others8%

This approach promotes inclusion, cohesion, and aligns with Uganda’s CRRF framework.

Your Support Transforms Lives

Help us expand access to education, healthcare, and livelihoods.
